message.content:
This parent section (pages 7–8, with detailed breakdowns spanning pages 8–15) delivers a comprehensive, data-driven analysis of 2025 global mobile game marketing trends, structured to support precise retrieval queries on market scale, creative adoption, genre performance, regional intensity, and platform allocation. 

**Topics & Entities:** Covers global mobile game advertising dynamics, new creative placement, genre/category marketing shifts, regional campaign intensity, and OS/format distribution. Key entities include the ad intelligence platform 广大大 (data source), major regions (Europe, North America, Southeast Asia, South America, HK/Macau/TW, Japan/Korea, Oceania, etc.), OS platforms (iOS, Android), ad channels (Facebook, X/Twitter, UnityAds), and game genres (RPG, Strategy, Casino/Entertainment, Casual, Puzzle, Simulation, Action, Arcade, Adventure, Card, Board, Text).

**Dates:** Primary coverage spans January–December 2025, with year-over-year (YoY) comparisons to 2024 and three-year trend analysis (2023–2025).

**Key Claims & Metrics:** 
- *Market Scale & Growth:* Monthly active advertisers average >84,000 (peaking >90,000 in June), reflecting 21.9% YoY growth. New advertisers average ~8,000/month (9.4% share), with an annual new advertiser share of 23.4%.
- *Creative Adoption:* >82% of advertisers deploy new creatives monthly (avg 82.5%, +14.6% YoY). New creative share averages 58.1% (+5.9pp YoY), surpassing 60% in November and peaking at 63.2% in December.
- *Genre Performance:* RPG leads in ad volume (avg 801 creatives/product annually), followed by Strategy (728). Casino/Entertainment shows the highest growth, with advertiser share at 47% (+22.7% YoY) and creative share at 22.3% (+13.9% YoY).
- *Regional Intensity:* Europe is the only region exceeding 40,000 monthly average advertisers (46.2K). HK/Macau/TW leads in monthly creative volume (122), followed by North America (119).
- *Format & OS Allocation:* Video creatives dominate at 74.1% (+14.2% YoY), with <30s duration accounting for 58%. Square images lead at 67.2%. Android holds 79.6% of advertisers and 77.3% of creatives overall. Heavier game genres correlate with higher iOS creative shares (Heavy: ~32.6–35.6%), while mainstream channels allocate >70% to Android (X/Twitter shows the highest iOS share at 46.6%).

**Tables & Figures:** Features monthly 2025 trend charts, 2023–2025 comparative trend lines, time-series line charts for new creative adoption, ranked lists/charts for genre creative volume, comparative bar charts for regional advertiser/creative metrics, categorical data tables for market share and YoY shifts, and OS/platform distribution charts.

**Evidence Types:** All claims and metrics are substantiated by backend scraped ad intelligence data, statistical aggregation, YoY comparative analysis, and categorical percentage breakdowns compiled by 广大大. No formal definitions or procedural workflows are detailed.

**Retrieval Utility:** Optimized to answer precise queries on 2025 advertiser volume/growth trajectories, creative format/duration benchmarks, genre-specific ad volumes, regional marketing intensity, OS allocation by game weight/channel, and underlying data methodologies/timeframes.

This parent section (pages 7–8, with detailed breakdowns spanning pages 8–15) delivers a comprehensive, data-driven analysis of 2025 global mobile game marketing trends, structured to support precise retrieval queries on market scale, creative adoption, genre performance, regional intensity, and platform allocation. 

**Topics & Entities:** Covers global mobile game advertising dynamics, new creative placement, genre/category marketing shifts, regional campaign intensity, and OS/format distribution. Key entities include the ad intelligence platform 广大大 (data source), major regions (Europe, North America, Southeast Asia, South America, HK/Macau/TW, Japan/Korea, Oceania, etc.), OS platforms (iOS, Android), ad channels (Facebook, X/Twitter, UnityAds), and game genres (RPG, Strategy, Casino/Entertainment, Casual, Puzzle, Simulation, Action, Arcade, Adventure, Card, Board, Text).

**Dates:** Primary coverage spans January–December 2025, with year-over-year (YoY) comparisons to 2024 and three-year trend analysis (2023–2025).

**Key Claims & Metrics:** 
- *Market Scale & Growth:* Monthly active advertisers average >84,000 (peaking >90,000 in June), reflecting 21.9% YoY growth. New advertisers average ~8,000/month (9.4% share), with an annual new advertiser share of 23.4%.
- *Creative Adoption:* >82% of advertisers deploy new creatives monthly (avg 82.5%, +14.6% YoY). New creative share averages 58.1% (+5.9pp YoY), surpassing 60% in November and peaking at 63.2% in December.
- *Genre Performance:* RPG leads in ad volume (avg 801 creatives/product annually), followed by Strategy (728). Casino/Entertainment shows the highest growth, with advertiser share at 47% (+22.7% YoY) and creative share at 22.3% (+13.9% YoY).
- *Regional Intensity:* Europe is the only region exceeding 40,000 monthly average advertisers (46.2K). HK/Macau/TW leads in monthly creative volume (122), followed by North America (119).
- *Format & OS Allocation:* Video creatives dominate at 74.1% (+14.2% YoY), with <30s duration accounting for 58%. Square images lead at 67.2%. Android holds 79.6% of advertisers and 77.3% of creatives overall. Heavier game genres correlate with higher iOS creative shares (Heavy: ~32.6–35.6%), while mainstream channels allocate >70% to Android (X/Twitter shows the highest iOS share at 46.6%).

**Tables & Figures:** Features monthly 2025 trend charts, 2023–2025 comparative trend lines, time-series line charts for new creative adoption, ranked lists/charts for genre creative volume, comparative bar charts for regional advertiser/creative metrics, categorical data tables for market share and YoY shifts, and OS/platform distribution charts.

**Evidence Types:** All claims and metrics are substantiated by backend scraped ad intelligence data, statistical aggregation, YoY comparative analysis, and categorical percentage breakdowns compiled by 广大大. No formal definitions or procedural workflows are detailed.

**Retrieval Utility:** Optimized to answer precise queries on 2025 advertiser volume/growth trajectories, creative format/duration benchmarks, genre-specific ad volumes, regional marketing intensity, OS allocation by game weight/channel, and underlying data methodologies/timeframes.
